items.speaker: Abby
items.text: All four Melody Boxes are marked with different sigils… Know what they mean, {PlayerName}?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: No. They don't look like they belong to any of the nobles either.
Well, well, if it isn't {PlayerName}, the famous Champion and Laureate!
Got yourself into some trouble?

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Hello there! It's been a while!

options.text: You're that journalist we met at Maroonwood.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Haha, didn't expect the Laureate to still remember me after our short encounter.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: It's understandable if you don't remember me. It was a short encounter after all.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Back in Maroonwood, I was busy encouraging those two young Gladiators into a friendly duel. Didn't notice it was you passing by until it was too late.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Guess I'm lucky to bump into you again today. Otherwise, I'd have lived the rest of my life with regret.

options.text: Something I can help you with?

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Right. I'm not sure why you'd go through all this trouble helping an Echo, but you're stuck and need clues, yes?

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Not many Septimontians recognize these sigils… Except me, that is. I can help you in exchange for a tiny favor.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Relax, it's nothing over-the-top. An exclusive interview with you will do.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: I won't take too much of your time, and we can do the interview after solving your Echo problem first. How's that sound?

options.text: Exclusive interview? Then I'll need exclusive pay.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Decisive! As expected from the Champion and Laureate!

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: By all means. I'll make sure to book the quietest room in the fanciest hotel in Septimont for our interview.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Alright, let's take care of that delivery of yours first. Trust me, I know Rinascita like the back of my hand.

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Pierrot takes the Melody Boxes from the Aria Mummer and points at the sigils.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: These sigils refer to four different locations. But since Septimont City relocated uphill a while back, so too did the people who used to live there.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Don't worry, I'll help you locate these Melody Box recipients.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: They should have all moved close to the hilltop Resonance Beacon. It'll be easy. Shall we?

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: The third recipient lives here.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: They're quite a renowned family in Septimont. Their eldest son, Galba, is a veteran of the Hunter's Den.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: I sent them a message to let them know we were coming, but they never replied… Please don't tell me this is going to be like the last two recipients...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: You know, that big mouth of yours is gonna get you in trouble sooner or later.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Don't you worry. Journalists move fast and are hard to hit.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Wait, the door's opening. A medic. I'll go talk to her and see what I can find.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: That makes the final box. Anything else you wanna do? Let us know.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hmm… It says it wants to go back to its master's home and meet her brother, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: But its travelled with its master for so long, it's not sure if its master's family moved some place else or not…

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Strozzia… Strozzia… Now that we have her name, it makes things easy. I know basically everyone in Ragunna.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: You're smarter than you look. Shocker!

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Excuse me?! Does this Echo of yours specialize in words that inflict emotional damage, {PlayerName}?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hey! I'm right here, you know? Show this Echo some respect!

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: My apologies, I don't usually talk to coffee machines or phonographs.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Give me a second. My colleague in Ragunna just replied! They've found the address of the Strozzia residence. They never moved.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: When did you even send that message?!

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Journalist's hidden ability. A trade secret. Anyway, let's go visit Ms. Strozzia's family.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: If we waste any more time, the Aria Mummer will be sent back to the Resonance Nexus.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Given Grisoni's current frequency strength, I wouldn't be surprised if it returns to the Nexus right this moment… It's holding on through willpower, like a human.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: To Ragunna.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: The Echo wasn't following orders from its master. It acted on its own, traveling from the Hunter's Den to Septimont City to make the deliveries, then back to Ragunna…

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: It's remarkable. I wonder if Echoes really have feelings of their own.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I can't say for sure, but I can share with you a true story...

options.text: (Tell Pierrot the story of Cartethyia and the Echoes.)
(Tell Pierrot the story of the Echoes from the Troupe of Fools.)

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Echoes that the Blessed Maiden grew up with still worried about her even after being corrupted by the Dark Tide… Incredible, indeed.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Echoes that thought they had been abandoned by their masters, yet still traveled to the farthest islands to find them… Incredible, indeed.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Well, I'm not saying I don't believe your story. I've even seen a talking Echo with my own eyes, after all.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: It's just… I suddenly get this feeling that this vast world I see might only be a tiny corner of an infinite universe.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: I'm planning to write an article about Strozzia and her team. And, of course, about her Echo.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: It'll take some time, so you'd better not forget the exclusive interview you promised me!

options.text: Thought I'd get away with it...

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: Great. I knew our Laureate wouldn't go back on {Male=his;Female=her} word.

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: I knew it! Don't you forget about it! I'll come find you once I'm done!

items.speaker: Pierrot
items.text: I've got work on this article now. Until next time! Don't forget to fit me into your busy schedule!
